title: The Shelter.
content:
The body grows outside, --
    The more convenient way, --
    That if the spirit like to hide,
    Its temple stands alway

    Ajar, secure, inviting;
    It never did betray
    The soul that asked its shelter
    In timid honesty.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "The Shelter.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
